chapter_title: Railway or tramway locomotives, rolling-stock and parts thereof; railway or tramway track fixtures and fittings and parts thereof; mechanical (including electro-mechanical) traffic signalling equipment of all kinds

inclusions.description: Rail locomotives powered from an external source of electricity or by electric accumulators
Self-propelled railway or tramway coaches, vans and trucks (other than maintenance or service vehicles)
Railway or tramway maintenance or service vehicles (e.g., workshops, cranes, ballast tampers, trackliners, testing coaches and track inspection vehicles)
Railway or tramway passenger coaches, luggage vans, post office coaches and other special-purpose railway/tramway coaches (not self-propelled); sleeping cars and dining cars
Railway or tramway goods vans and wagons (not self-propelled)

Parts of railway/tramway locomotives or rolling-stock, including: (a) axles, wheels, wheel sets (running gear), metal tyres, hoops, hubs and other wheel parts; (b) frames, underframes, bogies and bissel-bogies; (c) axle boxes; brake gear; (d) buffers for rolling-stock; hooks and other coupling gear and corridor connections; (e) coachwork

Railway or tramway track fixtures and fittings including: (a) assembled track, turntables, platform buffers, loading gauges; (b) semaphores, mechanical signal discs, level crossing control gear, signal and point controls, and other mechanical (including electro-mechanical) signalling, safety or traffic control equipment — whether or not fitted for electric lighting — for railways, tramways, roads, inland waterways, parking facilities, port installations or airfields
Containers (including containers for the transport of fluids) specially designed and equipped for carriage by one or more modes of transport

exclusions.condition: Railway or tramway sleepers of wood or of concrete, OR concrete guide-track sections for hovertrains
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 44.06 (wood sleepers) or 68.10 (concrete sleepers/guide-track sections) — Note 1(a)

exclusions.condition: Railway or tramway track construction material of iron or steel
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 73.02 (rails, check-rails, fish-plates, baseplates, rail clips, bedplates, ties of iron/steel) — Note 1(b)

exclusions.condition: Electrical signalling, safety or traffic control equipment
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.30 (electrical apparatus for signalling, safety or traffic control) — Note 1(c). Chapter 86 only covers MECHANICAL (including electro-mechanical) signalling equipment; purely ELECTRICAL signalling equipment → 85.30

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Mechanical vs. Electrical signalling equipment (86.08 vs. 85.30)
classification_rules.description: Heading 86.08 covers MECHANICAL (including electro-mechanical) signalling, safety or traffic control equipment — the mechanical action is primary. Heading 85.30 covers purely ELECTRICAL/ELECTRONIC signalling equipment. Test: if the signalling function is achieved primarily by electrical/electronic means → 85.30; if primarily mechanical (with possible electrical trigger or lighting) → 86.08. This applies to railways, roads, inland waterways, parking facilities, port installations and airfields.

Note 2 confirms heading 86.07 covers, inter alia: axles, wheels, wheel sets (running gear), metal tyres, hoops and hubs; frames, underframes, bogies and bissel-bogies; axle boxes; brake gear; buffers; hooks, coupling gear, corridor connections; coachwork. Classification here requires the part to be identifiable as specific to railway/tramway rolling-stock use; general-purpose parts that happen to be fitted → classified by constituent material/function.

classification_rules.rule: Heading 86.08 — track fixtures and mechanical signalling (broad scope)
classification_rules.description: Note 3 confirms heading 86.08 covers, inter alia: assembled track, turntables, platform buffers, loading gauges; semaphores, mechanical signal discs, level crossing control gear, signal and point controls, and other mechanical (including electro-mechanical) signalling equipment — whether or not fitted for electric lighting. Coverage extends beyond railways: roads, inland waterways, parking facilities, port installations and airfields are all included as application domains.

classification_rules.rule: Track construction material vs. track fixtures: 73.02 vs. 86.08
classification_rules.description: Heading 73.02 captures raw TRACK CONSTRUCTION MATERIAL of iron/steel (rails, check-rails, fish-plates, baseplates, rail clips, sleepers of metal). Heading 86.08 captures ASSEMBLED TRACK FIXTURES AND FITTINGS — i.e., functional assemblies ready for installation (assembled track sections, turntables, platform buffers). Individual steel rail components presented unassembled → 73.02. Assembled/functional track fixtures → 86.08.

Chapter 86 Note 1 creates three explicit redirects out of Chapter 86: (1) wood sleepers / concrete sleepers → Ch.44 or 68.10; (2) iron/steel track construction materials → 73.02; (3) electrical signalling equipment → 85.30. These exclusions take absolute priority over any apparent functional connection to railways/tramways.
